title: Fruit of Knowledge
wikitext:
The '''Fruit of Knowledge''', as opposed to the [[S² Engine|Fruit of Life]] which the [[Angel|Angels]] possess, is the Fruit which the [[Lilim]] possess.

During episode 12 Gendo Ikari states that ''"science is the power of man,"'' possibly suggesting that the Fruit of Knowledge is man's ability to cogitate, using his knowledge of the universe to build the Evangelions, which Kozo Fuyutsuki says mankind was destined to build during [[End of Evangelion]].

{{stub}}

[[Category:A to Z]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
[[Category:Religious and Esoteric References]]
html:
The Fruit of Knowledge, as opposed to the Fruit of Life which the Angels possess, is the Fruit which the Lilim possess.

During episode 12 Gendo Ikari states that "science is the power of man," possibly suggesting that the Fruit of Knowledge is man's ability to cogitate, using his knowledge of the universe to build the Evangelions, which Kozo Fuyutsuki says mankind was destined to build during End of Evangelion.


This article bears the unpleasant designation of "Stub." This is a Bad Thing. Provide a great service to NGE fan-geeks everywhere by making it awesomer!
summary: The Fruit of Knowledge, as opposed to the Fruit of Life which the Angels possess, is the Fruit which the Lilim possess.

chunks.content:
The Fruit of Knowledge, as opposed to the Fruit of Life which the Angels possess, is the Fruit which the Lilim possess.

During episode 12 Gendo Ikari states that "science is the power of man," possibly suggesting that the Fruit of Knowledge is man's ability to cogitate, using his knowledge of the universe to build the Evangelions, which Kozo Fuyutsuki says mankind was destined to build during End of Evangelion.
